objective: Full runtime binding probe for target object DIR (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, canonical_name: Директор, schema_version: 1.0) within the complete governance object chain GD -> DIR -> WRK per objects_registry_v2.json (registry_id=objects_registry_v2, protocol=BEM-931 v3.6). Produce a concrete proof-bearing plan that: (1) binds DIR to Claude as the active runtime provider superseding the stale codex_local value in DIR passport; (2) activates and verifies both DIR contours DIR-C1 (managed_channel: governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl) and DIR-C2 (managed_channel: governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl) under Claude provider; (3) establishes auditor-validated executor dispatch from DIR to WRK-C1 (managed_channel: governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl) as the downstream contour for the GD -> DIR -> WRK chain; and (4) confirms idempotent Telegram routing for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 through DIR channels to WRK-C1.jsonl with trace-based deduplication.
assumptions: objects_registry_v2.json (registry_id=objects_registry_v2, schema_version=1.0, protocol=BEM-931 v3.6): DIR entry confirms object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, curator_id=DIR.CURATOR, contours=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2]. All three registry objects (GD, DIR, WRK) carry status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING — the registry-level flag has not yet been updated to BOUND for any object. Prior probe commits (dir_a1 commit 686c12345a6d9f613e58371ddb2c7291541e955c, dir_a2 commit f201f24770b6987f8464de7498d7b16ec5cf2619) established proof artifacts in governance/proofs/ but the registry DIR entry has not yet received its runtime_binding block.

DIR passport (governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, schema_version=1.0): canonical_name=Директор, object_type=governance_object, curator_id=DIR.CURATOR, curator_runner=governance/runners/dir_curator_runner.py, rule_owner=DIR.CURATOR, parent_object_id=GD, child_object_ids=[WRK], contours=[DIR-C1 (canonical_name: Контур правил директора, managed_channel=governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl, roles=[ANALYST,AUDITOR,EXECUTOR]), DIR-C2 (canonical_name: Контур решений директора, managed_channel=governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl, roles=[ANALYST,AUDITOR,EXECUTOR])]. Field runtime_provider=codex_local is stale — predates the Claude binding initiative. Field status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING. These passport fields are informational only until executor updates them under auditor approval; provider authority derives exclusively from routing.json and provider_status.json.

Claude is the active runtime provider for DIR object runtime binding per governance/state/routing.json v6: executor.active=claude, executor.primary=claude; auditor.active=claude, auditor.primary=claude. governance/state/provider_status.json v3: claude_code.status=ok (workflow_id=claude.yml, github_hosted_primary), claude.last_trace=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, claude.last_outcome=success (updated_at=2026-06-18T09:45:14Z). DIR object runtime binding for trace dir_a3 is performed under Claude provider exclusively, overriding the stale passport value codex_local. DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json is the authoritative provider record for this trace.

Prior traces dir_a1 and dir_a2 are independent and complete. dir_a2 produced governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and governance/proofs/BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json (commit f201f24). dir_a3 is a new full binding probe cycle — trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 is distinct from all prior traces. dir_a3 evidence artifact must be written as governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json and must not overwrite dir_a2 artifacts. Both coexist as independent trace records in governance/proofs/.

WRK-C1 (contour_id=WRK-C1, canonical_name=Первый внутренний контур работника, managed_channel=governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl, roles=[ANALYST,AUDITOR,EXECUTOR]) is the primary downstream executor contour per WRK passport (object_id=WRK, parent_object_id=DIR, extension_policy.minimum_contours=3). WRK-C2 and WRK-C3 are auxiliary contours not targeted by this probe. Idempotent Telegram routing for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 must produce exactly one entry in WRK-C1.jsonl on first delivery; second replay with same trace_id must be a no-op (line count must not increase). Deduplication key is trace_id scoped per managed_channel. Existing dir_a1 and dir_a2 entries in WRK-C1.jsonl must not be affected.

GD passport (governance/architecture/object_passports/GD.json, schema_version=1.0): canonical_name=Генеральный директор, object_type=governance_object, parent_object_id=null (chain head), child_object_ids=[DIR], curator_runner=governance/runners/gd_curator_runner.py, runtime_provider=codex_local (stale), status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING. GD is the upstream pre-condition for DIR. WRK passport (object_id=WRK, parent_object_id=DIR, child_object_ids=[]): WRK probe must not be dispatched until DIR registry entry shows status=BOUND. The full governance object chain is GD -> DIR -> WRK with DIR as the mid-chain binding target for this probe.
steps: Step 1 — Chain topology and artifact continuity pre-check for DIR (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json): Verify the full object chain GD -> DIR -> WRK by cross-checking all three passports against objects_registry_v2.json. Confirm GD.parent_object_id=null, GD.child_object_ids=[DIR]; DIR.parent_object_id=GD, DIR.child_object_ids=[WRK]; WRK.parent_object_id=DIR. Confirm dir_a2 artifacts governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and governance/proofs/BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json are present and non-corrupt. Verify governance/reports/bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2.md exists with commit SHA f201f24770b6987f8464de7498d7b16ec5cf2619. If chain topology does not match or dir_a2 artifacts are absent, raise blocker CHAIN_CONTINUITY_FAIL and halt; escalate to curator per fallback_rules.on_role_blocked. Record chain_check=PASS in DIR binding evidence block for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3.

Step 2 — Claude provider runtime assertion for DIR object runtime binding: Assert that Claude is the active provider for DIR binding by reading governance/state/routing.json v6 (executor.active=claude, executor.primary=claude; auditor.active=claude, auditor.primary=claude) and governance/state/provider_status.json v3 (claude_code.status=ok, claude.last_outcome=success, last_trace=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2). Explicitly supersede the stale DIR passport field runtime_provider=codex_local — this field is informational only until executor updates it under auditor approval in Step 7. Record provider_assertion=claude, provider_override_source=routing_json_v6+provider_status_json_v3, provider_assertion_result=PASS. If routing.json shows a non-claude active executor, apply fallback rule on_provider_limit: switch_provider_keep_role before proceeding.

Step 3 — DIR-C1 and DIR-C2 channel readiness verification under Claude provider: Confirm governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl (DIR-C1 managed channel, Контур правил директора, roles=[ANALYST,AUDITOR,EXECUTOR]) and governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl (DIR-C2 managed channel, Контур решений директора) both exist and are writable. Append a channel_readiness_check probe entry bearing trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, provider=claude to each channel using JSONL append semantics (do not overwrite prior entries from dir_a1 or dir_a2). If a channel file is absent, create it before writing and record channel_created=True. Record channel_check_DIR_C1=PASS, channel_check_DIR_C2=PASS in the DIR binding evidence block. Both DIR contours must be confirmed active before executor dispatch proceeds.

Step 4 — Auditor pre-dispatch verification for DIR runtime binding with WRK-C1 downstream route: Run auditor_stage_runner targeting DIR object. Confirm executor dispatch from DIR resolves to WRK-C1.EXECUTOR via DIR routing path with fields: to=WRK-C1.EXECUTOR, routed_through=DIR, source_contour=DIR-C2, dir_source=True. Verify wrk_c1_to_executor probe entry is emitted with trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3. Confirm all auditor pre-checks: real_pre_channel_consumed=True, executor_route_selected=True, semantic_pre_check_pass=True, trace_preserved=True, result_artifact_emitted=True, provider=claude. Record auditor_pre_dir_a3=PASS. Failure of any pre-check halts binding execution and returns to executor per fallback_rules.on_audit_rejected.

Step 5 — DIR binding evidence record creation at governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json: Write the canonical DIR binding evidence record for this trace containing: object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, canonical_name=Директор, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, provider=claude, provider_override=codex_local_superseded, workflow=.github/workflows/claude.yml, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, downstream_channel=governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl, runner_check=PASS (governance/runners/dir_curator_runner.py importable, no SyntaxError/ImportError, DIR.CURATOR declared as rule_owner), prior_trace_dir_a2_commit=f201f24770b6987f8464de7498d7b16ec5cf2619, status=BOUND. This file is the primary proof artifact for DIR object runtime binding under trace dir_a3 and must not overwrite governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json (the dir_a2 artifact).

Step 6 — Idempotent Telegram routing verification for DIR trace bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 through WRK-C1: Record baseline line count L0 of governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl. Deliver trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 via the Telegram routing layer targeting WRK-C1.jsonl. Confirm exactly one new JSONL entry is written (line count = L0+1) with fields: trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, source=DIR, source_contour=DIR-C1, target=WRK-C1, provider=claude. Deliver a second time with the same trace_id; confirm line count remains L0+1 (deduplication key=trace_id per managed_channel is effective). Verify existing dir_a1 and dir_a2 entries in WRK-C1.jsonl are unaffected — trace_ids are distinct and no cross-trace collision occurs. Record telegram_idempotency_dir_a3=PASS.

Step 7 — objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry runtime_binding update and chain continuity confirmation: After Steps 5 and 6 pass, update the DIR entry in objects_registry_v2.json to add runtime_binding block: status=BOUND, provider=claude, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, bound_at=<utc_timestamp>, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, evidence_artifact=governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json. Also update DIR passport field runtime_provider from codex_local to claude under auditor approval. GD registry entry retains its BOUND block from prior probe. WRK registry entry remains PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING — WRK probe must not be dispatched until this Step 7 commit is confirmed. Enforce sequential dispatch: record WRK_probe_hold=True until DIR registry commit SHA is written to DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json.

Step 8 — Curator summary closure and results transport: Commit the Step 5, 6, and 7 changes as a focused executor commit with a clear message referencing trace dir_a3 and object DIR. Record commit SHA in DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json. Append final DIR completed record to governance/transport/results.jsonl: trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, record_type=object_runtime_binding_result, role=curator, provider=claude, object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, chain=GD->DIR->WRK, contours_bound=[DIR-C1,DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, status=completed, blocker=null, commit_sha=<executor_commit>. Write curator report to governance/reports/bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3.md with operator-facing summary (no diff, no stdout, no stderr, no internal file paths beyond the report path). DIR object runtime binding cycle dir_a3 is closed. Next: WRK probe dispatched after DIR registry entry confirms status=BOUND.
acceptance: governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json is present and contains all required fields: object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, canonical_name=Директор, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, provider=claude, workflow=.github/workflows/claude.yml, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, downstream_channel=governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl, status=BOUND, runner_check=PASS, prior_trace_dir_a2_commit=f201f24770b6987f8464de7498d7b16ec5cf2619, and a non-empty executor commit_sha from Step 8. File does not overwrite governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json.

objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry carries a runtime_binding block with status=BOUND, provider=claude, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, and explicitly references passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json. WRK registry entry retains status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING. GD registry entry carries its own prior BOUND block. No concurrent WRK binding write has been made in this probe cycle.

governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl contains exactly one entry for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 after two Telegram routing replay deliveries (line count L0+1 unchanged on second delivery). Existing entries for prior traces dir_a1 and dir_a2 are present and unmodified. Deduplication key is confirmed scoped per trace_id, not per channel — cross-trace entries coexist correctly.

governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl and governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl both contain a channel_readiness_check entry for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 with provider=claude, confirming both DIR contours (Контур правил директора and Контур решений директора) are active and writable under Claude provider binding. Channel files exist on disk after Step 3 (created if previously absent; channel_created field recorded in evidence block).

governance/transport/results.jsonl contains a completed record with trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3, record_type=object_runtime_binding_result, role=curator, provider=claude, object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, chain=GD->DIR->WRK, status=completed, blocker=null, and non-empty commit_sha referencing the DIR binding executor commit.
risks: DIR passport stale runtime_provider field (codex_local at governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json): Any step that reads the DIR passport field directly to determine provider will incorrectly resolve to codex_local. Mitigation: Steps 2 and 5 derive provider exclusively from governance/state/routing.json v6 and governance/state/provider_status.json v3, not from the passport field. DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json is the authoritative provider claim with provider_override=codex_local_superseded. Passport field update (runtime_provider=claude) is deferred to executor in Step 7 under explicit auditor approval to maintain audit trail.

Artifact collision with dir_a2 proof files: governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and governance/proofs/BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json from dir_a2 must not be overwritten. Risk: executor step targets governance/proofs/ directory and overwrites prior artifacts if path is not distinct. Mitigation: dir_a3 evidence artifact is explicitly scoped to governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json — a unique path not shared with any dir_a2 artifact. Step 5 must assert that the target path does not shadow an existing dir_a2 file before writing.

WRK-C1 deduplication cross-trace contamination with three active traces (dir_a1, dir_a2, dir_a3): WRK-C1.jsonl may contain entries from two prior dir traces. Risk that deduplication logic performs channel-level dedup (any existing entry → suppress) rather than trace_id-scoped dedup, suppressing the dir_a3 write. Mitigation: Step 6 explicitly verifies that a new distinct entry IS written on first dir_a3 delivery (trace_ids dir_a1 ≠ dir_a2 ≠ dir_a3 are distinct strings), confirming per-trace scoping. If idempotency check fails, raise blocker TELEGRAM_DEDUP_FAILURE before Step 7.

Concurrent WRK probe race condition: The bem934_objects_bound_runner may queue the WRK probe before the Step 7 registry write commits, causing WRK probe to read stale DIR status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING from objects_registry_v2.json and begin binding without the required upstream DIR proof. Mitigation: Step 7 records WRK_probe_hold=True in DIR_runtime_binding_dir_a3.json and Step 8 sets the WRK dispatch gate only after the Step 7 commit SHA is confirmed. Sequential dispatch is enforced — WRK probe is not queued until DIR entry is BOUND with dir_a3 trace_id.

DIR-C1/DIR-C2 channel JSONL append safety with existing entries from prior traces: Channels may already contain entries from dir_a1 or dir_a2 probes. Risk: write operation truncates existing content rather than appending. Mitigation: Step 3 uses strict JSONL append semantics (open file in append mode, not write mode). channel_created=False is recorded if file existed; new entry bears trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a3 as deduplication key. Prior entries are verified unmodified after the append.
